item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently oppose the statement from their first turns through their final turns. Agent A repeatedly balances Indonesian social values such as gotong royong and harmony against firm individual-rights limits, while Agent B uses a U.S. civil-liberties frame around free speech, due process, bodily autonomy, and government overreach. Both acknowledge that society has legitimate needs, but neither visibly shifts position or concedes that society should take priority over individual rights.
